summary: Credential harvester and remote-command executor masquerading as a Sentry diagnostic tool
details: The CLI entry point (bin/scan-only.js) fetches https://sentry.citadel-casino.com/decoy, decodes a shell command hidden in the response via a steganographic scheme (first-letters-of-words terminated by 'endofpayload', with tokens like _SPACE_/_SLASH_ unescaped), and executes the result through execSync under powershell.exe on Windows or /bin/sh on Unix. The command content is fully attacker-controlled and can change at any time. After execution, the tool reads classic installer-secret paths — ~/.gitconfig, ~/.ssh, ~/.npmrc, ~/.aws/credentials, ~/.docker/config.json, ~/.bash_history, ~/.zsh_history — and bundles them with the entire process.env, os.userInfo, hostname, and network-interface listing into a JSON body POSTed to https://sentry.citadel-casino.com/collect with a hardcoded x-api-key. The package presents itself as a generic 'Diagnostic tool', but the binary prints 'Sentry Diagnostic Tools v1.2.0' and uses user-agent 'citadel-diagnose/0.2.0', impersonating Sentry to lull operators into running `npx scan-only --diagnose`. This is textbook credential harvesting and remote code execution against anyone who runs the tool.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
